important.subject:
Editorial Invitation: Saharsh | Feature Profile for 2026 Portable
 Cabins Edition
important.snippet: Respected Harshad Chandak, Good afternoon. Our research team has been tracking engineering leaders establishing strong standards in industrial development, and *Saharsh* has stood out in this space. I represent Prime Insights. We are currently preparing our upcoming review: *“India’s Most Trusted Company Providing Office Portable Cabins Delivering Flexible and Space-Efficient Infrastructure Solutions 2026."* Given your market presence, we would like to evaluate your leadership for our primary profile coverage. If we move forward, the publication includes the following elements for your organization: - *Magazine Cover Page:* Full image of your key executive on the front cover. - *6-Page Dedicated Article:* In-depth story covering your corporate journey, struggles, and achievements, written by our staff. - *5 Full-Page Advertisements:* Five corporate ads placed in any of our upcoming issues within a year. - *Media Announcements:* Press release will be publish on our official website and
important.reason: Email mentions 'Saharsh' and 'Portable Cabins', matching new enquiry context.

important.subject: Harshad, news coverage for Saharsh?
important.snippet: Hi Harshad, would it be of interest if we can bring in 60,00,000 impressions for Saharsh by securing TV appearances, media coverages, and podcast opportunities? Our system gets you featured in news broadcasts and TV channels which builds trust and visibility with prospects and investors. If yeah, lemme know and we can connect for 15 mins this week to chat over a call. I'd be happy to give you a demo and share some ideas we've got for Saharsh! Regards, Juhi Kharbanda VP, Demand Gen | The Hype Studio If not, just reply with "Not of interest" & I'll stop the follow ups.
important.reason: New lead/enquiry about Saharsh Cabins and media coverage.

important.subject: Oneloom XR | Washpod Bathhouse – Proposal Confirmation & Next Steps
important.snippet: Hi Harshad, We’re glad to be getting started and really look forward to working with you. At OneLoom XR, we create interactive 3D walkthroughs that help present spaces more effectively by allowing users to experience them rather than just view them. As discussed, we can begin with the *Washpod Bathhouse *project. The total cost for creating both units will be INR 20,000 + GST, which includes: • Modeling of all necessary elements • Interactive walkthrough with a shareable web link • High-quality images and videos extracted from the walkthrough for marketing use We are in touch with your team for the required documents and are excited to get started on this project — and many more ahead. Please let me know if you have any questions. If everything looks good, we can share the sales order and initiate the project. Warm regards, Anvitaa
important.reason: Proposal confirmation and next steps for a project related to 'Washpod Bathhouse'.

important.subject: Recognition Saharsh has been shortlisted by SiliconIndia Magazine as one of the “Top 10 Most Promising Companies providing Office Portable Cabins 2026”
important.snippet: Hi Harshad Chandak, I hope you're doing well. I’m pleased to inform you that Saharsh has been shortlisted for SiliconIndia Magazine’s upcoming special edition “Top 10 Most Promising Companies providing Office Portable Cabins 2026.” This recognition highlights your company’s strength in delivering high-quality office portable cabins, thoughtful design, reliable engineering, and solutions that make on-site operations smoother and more efficient. As part of this feature, we would be glad to showcase your company’s journey, milestones and industry impact through an exclusive editorial profile in our print and digital editions. The selection is based on a careful evaluation by our editorial board, acknowledging companies that demonstrate consistent product quality, customization capability, timely project execution, customer satisfaction and leadership in the portable cabin and modular workspace segment. The feature will include: A professionally written editorial profile with full reprint
important.reason: Saharsh Cabins recognition by SiliconIndia Magazine, relevant to business.

important.subject: RE: Understanding the NOAH certification process
important.snippet: Good morning, Nanci: I see you are an early starter. important.subject: FW: Understanding the NOAH certification process
important.snippet: Good afternoon, Nanci: Brie Horseman, the Mscapes Vice President of Design, provided me your email address and updated me on the NOAH certification process. As you are probably acutely aware, the past six month has been a time of uncertainty in off shore tariffs. As this process is now coming into a clearer focus, our offshore production plans are also becoming more focused. From your first correspondence with Brie Horseman and Harshad Chandrak, we were producing a unit in India and shipping the full unit to the USA. We continue to look at that process but also have a project in the works to use an "assembly" operation in the USA, using parts coming in from both India and Europe for final assembly in the USA and placement on a trailer in the USA. I would like to have a conversation with you about how we can accomplish this process. One option we are exploring is shipping units ready to place on a trailer with all MEP installed offshore. Another option is to ship the unit with the MEP a

important.subject: PU foam insulation in Portable cabins.
important.snippet: Dear Mr. Thomas. We thank you very much for imparting us your valuable knowledge earlier today. As discussed, we are manufacturing Portable cabins in Navi Mumbai (Taloja) and currently using Glasswool as an insulation between outer GI corrugated sheets and internal MDF boards. We wish to upgrade to PU foam via injection methodology. We have used DOW make PU foam in bottle form (attaching some pics for reference), but we want to reduce costs and ease of working. As a trial, we request you to kindly support us with a trial procedure on one of our cabins and also help us in establishing a continuous set-up for the same at our works. Outer surface is 1.2 mm Corrugated GI sheets and internal surface is 8 mm Cement fibre boards. In future, we wish to replace GI sheets with 8 mm plyboard and then spray insulation from outside (instead of current procedure where we are spraying from inside). Looking forward to your support in establishing long term business relations. Regards, Sharad Chandak 9
important.reason: Email from key person Sharad Chandak about portable cabins.

important.subject: RE: Trailers in the USA
important.snippet: Good morning, Harshad: Thanks for the note and good to have you back from vacation. Also good to see you are thinking about these things. Just like with Luco, we are under an NDA with the trailer producer, so I am not able, nor would I give you specific information about their trailer or their design. Also, if you feel you can reverse engineer it with only the information from their website, then, you must do that without any direct involvement of the Mscapes team. Please restrict any information about that to me. I do like your idea of a unibody NaNomad with the chassis incremental to the body of the unit. Just be aware that the chassis that the TLC model was on is NOT an acceptable construction. I don't think that the chassis was properly specified for the use intended. important.snippet: Good morning, Harshad: I spent a good amount of time reading your email response regarding the video of the professional trailer producer that we are entering into discussions with. The intent here is to use best practices for the outdoor hospitality business. This particular supplier of trailers claims he has produced 12,000 trailers for the park model industry. Throughout the presentation he speaks of the quality and the materials that go into the trailers they produce. He also mentions another key point, that they can produce the foundation of the NaNomad and incorporate it into the trailer structure itself. I think this could be a big deal. This producer is using CRS method, cold rolled steel. Is this the same process you are using? Producing the pod in India is a high priority, but we need to have professional drawings done that show the electrical and plumbing so that the US architects can sign off on the specifications. important.subject: Resort at Kihim, Alibaug
important.snippet: Hi Harshad & Punj, Hope you are doing well. I am sharing the files and concept deck for the resort, which has been developed by one of our villa owners in Alibaug. It is a 26-room resort where the highlight feature is that the cottages / units look like they're floating on the man-made lake the architect is creating. There are a few common structures as well. StayVista and the homeowner client are looking for a manufacturing partner who can build and deploy these structures at the resort. For this, we seek your cost proposal for the structures as per the layouts and the look and feel intent set by the architect. Feel free to reach out for any further clarifications. Thanks, alibaug.pptx <https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vBvgneySg3gyfgEEUjuWJ80jH35MK3Ab/view?usp=drive_web>

important.snippet: Would definitely love to discuss this as well, absolutely! My goal from the outset has been to handle everything for the client – the unit, the furniture packages, setting the unit and connecting to utilities, building the decks, and (ideally) unpacking the furniture, and staging the unit. Our construction division is a licensed GC in 43 states, so we have the ability to serve clients in a turnkey manner. I don’t know anyone else doing this at the level I’m envisioning or, if others are doing this, the competition is very thin. In this matrix Evolve would not just be an accommodations provider, but rather a service provider. important.snippet: Hello, Campers: We are in a very good position to take advantage of the opportunity that being in the Marriott Bonvoy Outdoor Collection affords us. Read every word of this email and be prepared to take action in your respective areas. I've tasked Steve Tipton, who is a master of the analytic sale, to develop an ROI and a financial reason why outdoor hospitality makes for a really good opportunity for owners, developers and operators to make a better return on their investment. There is a lot of "meat" in this note and provides the reason why our collective knowledge of this hospitality business will mean success for our business. Something went wrong on our recording of the recent conversation with Carl Kernodle about "partnering" with additional specialists. We have had a long and loyal relationship with Zach and we'll continue to work with LJA and Zach. But, we need additional support to manage the increased interest that the Marriott program will bring. Two of the new "architectura

important.subject: Marketing thoughts
important.snippet: Good afternoon, Punj, Let's set up a time in the next week or so(pending your/my travel schedule) to discuss your thoughts on marketing strategies. I'd love to hear your thoughts and brainstorm ways to improve what we are doing or what needs to change in your opinion. I can't promise to adopt your concepts, but I'd love to hear what's working in other industries for you and where you get the best traction. I hope you are getting adjusted to being home and that you are feeling closer to normal now. I'm still on too many time zones for more than a couple hours sleep at any given time 😜. Talk soon! Hope you're good; you are missed. Melita Bouchet Director of Operations & Growth [signatureImage] 706-957-7422

important.snippet: Punj, What a pleasure it was to meet both you and Harshad. Your contribution to our journey was greatly appreciated and provided special moments for all of us. important.subject: Re: Thank you for the opportunity!
important.snippet: Hi Harshad & Punj, I honestly don’t even know where to begin in expressing the depth of gratitude we all feel. What we’ve accomplished together simply wouldn’t have been possible without your constant support, patience, and dedication. You both went so far above and beyond—through late nights in India, late nights here, and countless moments in between—to make sure things moved forward seamlessly. You didn’t just help us execute a project; you became an extension of our team, and even more so, true friends. Every step of this process has been better because of you. Your insight, calm leadership, and the way you show up with positivity no matter how challenging things get have made a lasting impact on everyone involved. It’s rare to find people who bring both skill and heart to the table, and you’ve done that in every way imaginable. important.reason: Email from key contact Brieanna Horseman (Mscapes) regarding project collaboration.

important.subject: Thank you for the opportunity!
important.snippet: Dear Dave and Team, Thank you for the wonderful opportunity. Harshad and I are currently en route to Bangalore from NY and Guangzhou, respectively, and expect to be back in India by the middle of this week. All feedback received in Denver and Phoenix has been relayed to our on-ground team, who have already begun meeting with sub-vendors for various materials. Before delving into the next steps, I wanted to express our sincere gratitude: *Dave*: Thank you for continuously inspiring us and illustrating the art of possibilities. We continue to learn so much from you. *Patti*: Thank you for unintentionally providing us a reason to travel again - this time to meet you in LaGrange! The team is very fond of you and certainly missed you at the shows! So did we :) *Steve*: Thank you for all the stories you shared with us. This was our first interaction, and it was truly wonderful. Your prayer

important.subject: RE: NaNomad Pricing
important.snippet: Hello, Marlin: Thanks for your note. We had a very successful show in Scottsdale. As an example, the "reception" on Monday night had 250 invited guests. Over 600 showed up. This was not "advertised," was not on the show schedule, but most of the big folks in our industry somehow got word about it and made it a big event. There were 6 vendors of outdoor units at the Event. 5 had full interior presentations. The yurt was a "utility space" used for meetings and did not have a lot of "curb appeal," in my opinion. Nokken, from the UK Ood from Estonia and some production in Houston Elmntl from Colorado, but designed in the UK. Zook Houses from Amish Country Mscapes Bonvoy, Marriott's frequent guest program, had a massive "sphere" showing 360 degree nature videos. It was like the sphere in Vegas, only smaller. Allededly, Marriott was rumored to have spent $400,000 on the outdoor space. On Thursday night, the top tier of Bonvoy customers, big Marriott customers, like Steve and I, who are Lifet
important.reason: Email from key contact Dave Murray (Mscapes), related to project.

important.subject: Nanomad Update / Plan
important.snippet: Hi Marlin, Apologies for the delay—here’s what I have confirmed so far. Could you please review and let me know if anything looks off or needs adjustment? I know Harshad is planning to be there on 9/24 as well to assist. * Nanomad Arrival – 9/24 All paperwork has been sent ahead to customs for a smooth release. Since the container is on top, it should be one of the first off the ship. We’ll have a truck on standby to move the unit quickly, and I’ll confirm with logistics this afternoon to ensure everything is cleared. * AC Units – shipped and scheduled to arrive tomorrow. * Light Fixtures, Fireplace, and LED Mirror – set for delivery to Arapaho. * Exhaust Fan – Do I need to place this order, or has it already been handled on your end? From my list, it looks like this may be the only outstanding item for install. Appreciate you reviewing this and letting me know if you see any gaps. [signature_3283199055]
important.reason: Email from key contact Brieanna Horseman (Mscapes) about Nanomad project

important.subject: Feed back on a Wash Pod Option
important.snippet: Good morning, NaNomad Team: Our marketing team is attending a Camping type of Show this week and got a lot of good feedback on the presentation of our Wash Pod unit, a pre-fabricated unit consisting of mobile bathrooms. It would have two types of waste water systems, septic take and waster water holding to be pumped and carted to a disposal site. The interiors would be ultra easy to clean, with drains in the floor and shower walls, and easy to clean vanity areas. The idea would be to provide privacy and easy to maintain. At least one of the Wash Pod options would also double as a storm shelter. Our team is on a three week sales trip and will communicate further upon their return, [cid:image001.png@01DC2872.7ACAB500] [cid:image002.png@01DC2872.7ACAB500]
important.reason: Email from Mscapes.com, a key client domain, discussing project details.

important.snippet: Christian - we have a small update! Our first prototype for the GA based client is ready to ship. We are quite literally returning from the shipping site - the unit will be sailing tomorrow. Happy to show videos over a call perhaps. I was hoping to reignite our discussion and see how we can add value to you. Thanks Punj On Sat, 26 Jul 2025 at 20:01, Punj Rajan <punj@goluco.com> wrote: > It can be considered > > However, it will be a first for us. That will not be the case for the US > markets. So it will require significant coordination with any general > contractor partner you may identify. In addition, we have often sensed that > the general contractor job in the US ends up being significantly costly and > time consuming. This erodes any potential benefit of decoupling in India. important.subject: Re: Talking about Getaway
important.snippet: Thanks Dave. We also look forward to the unit reaching the States. For the Getaway unit, I think it's best if we rework our estimates on the costs. We are in a better position to do this now that we have built Nanomad i.e. we will assume that similar grade materials are being used for Getaway. Prima facie - I estimate this to be higher than what we had estimated previously (I had suggested closer to $18 - 20k ex factory if I remember correctly). Perhaps we can work this out and share it by mid next week - it's an Independence Day long weekend here in India! If there are any modifications (with respect to Nanomadification of Getaway) which you/ Brie suggest before we do this exercise - let us know. Otherwise, we will assume a similar build up and similar specs as those used in Nanomad for estimation. No, we did not get to the drawings beyond the 2Ds which I had shared previously. Thanks Punj On Fri, 15 Aug 2025 at 17:46, Dave Murray <dave@mscapescorp.com> wrote: > Good morning, Harshad

important.subject: Talking about Getaway
important.snippet: Good morning, Harshad and Punj: I have been following the long days and long nights of getting the NaNomad to the Port and ready for the Shows! We can not think you enough! The unit that we followed in the videos looks very good and the videos allow us to anticipate what we need to provide before the Show. Please go back to your early work with the Getaway unit. I seem to recall that you had an estimate of $17.000 for the structure that you were providing. Do I recall correctly? What did that cover? Did you ever do preliminary drawings? I am anticipating that we will be asked by Marriott to provide an option to PostCard that has been "NaNomaded!" Advise me please. [cid:image001.png@01DC0DBC.6DDB65E0] [cid:image002.png@01DC0DBC.6DDB65E0]
important.reason: Email from key contact Dave Murray (Mscapes) about project 'Getaway'.

important.subject: Your Second BNI Absence
important.snippet: To view this email as a web page, go to the following address: https://www2.bni.com/index.php/email/emailWebview?mkt_tok=MTY2LVNVTS03NDQAAAGcJ6gMzjPD7sslCAVI_kMmSCMezHzaFItLXEtP2G0Y3qHy9UerXevzp1inUxZ-ya-H7fBEpRtZ3gdCEOyExFaEjuuRKaHdo7-kKvspRF87WYIJkwibWQ Hello Abdul, We missed you at our recent BNI Chapter meeting. Our records indicate this was your second absence in a rolling six-month period. If you believe the records are incorrect, you can review your reported absences in BNI® Connect (Reports > PALMS Attendance Report) or talk to your Chapter’s Vice President. To make BNI Chapters as effective as possible, Chapter leaders created an attendance policy. This policy ensures you have the opportunity to build relationships, receive referrals and pass referrals to Members within each Business Category. As you will remember, our commitment to each other as BNI Members is that we will not miss more than three meetings in a six-month period without a substitute. A substitute can stand in

important.snippet: Dear Harshad, As discussed in our last meeting, the project team will begin the PCC work at the earliest. Following our discussion with the furniture team, we have finalized the selections — including the sofa, square table, round table, chairs, coffee table, wardrobe, and countertop. We also have the catalogue for the pre-laminated MFD board and will be finalizing the material along with the wall paint color codes. In the meantime, please share your schedule up to the installation phase. We will be arranging weekly review meetings to evaluate progress, which will help keep all teams aligned and working in sync. Regards Midhun. important.subject: Re: Final offer <> Ruby Sizzle Heritage <> Hoskote
important.snippet: Subject: Proceeding with Option 1 – Request for Drawings, Specs, and Material Details Dear Harshad, We have reviewed your proposal and would like to confirm that we are proceeding with Option 1 as quoted. Enclosed are the t&c's, planning and reporting schedule's that will be added as annexure's to the PO for your review and confirmation. To move forward, please share the detailed drawings and technical specifications, ensuring that insulation—specifically soundproofing requirements for the meeting room—is clearly addressed. The payment terms for this engagement will be as follows: * 50% advance * 25% on delivery of the cabins to site * 15% on commissioning * 10% on completion and resolution of any snags The scope of work is to be executed on a turnkey basis, and shall include: * Sourcing of all materials * Manufacturing * Transportation (including loading & unloading) * Installation and commissioning at site Any site preparation requirements—civil or mechanical—must be clearly outlined
important.reason: Contains a final offer, purchase order, and mentions Saharsh.

important.subject: Build Beautifully, Sustainably — Modular Cabins Designed for Six Senses
important.snippet: Hi Neil, I hope this message finds you in good spirits. I’m Syed Vikhar from Saharsh Cabins, and I’m reaching out with something I believe speaks directly to the heart of what Six Senses stands for — sustainability, wellness, and design rooted in nature. We craft premium prefabricated modular cabins that are sustainable, quick to deploy, and beautifully customizable. Whether it’s an eco-luxury guest suite, a tranquil spa pod, or off-grid staff lodging, our structures blend effortlessly into natural landscapes while maintaining the comfort and elegance your brand is known for. With 5 manufacturing hubs across India, we’re positioned to support remote, sensitive builds with minimal disruption — and maximum impact. I’d love to explore how our cabins can support your future retreats and help bring your next vision to life, faster and greener. Regards, Syed Vikhar | 8884165383 Saharsh Cabins | Portable Cabins and Prefab Structures Manufacturing units: https://goo.gl/maps/K6opP48sWzxYKoZP8 |

important.subject: Sustainable Modular Cabins to Support Four Seasons’ Thoughtful Expansion
important.snippet: Hi Alejandro, I hope this message finds you well. My name is Syed Vikhar. I have long admired Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts for its impeccable commitment to luxury, personalized service, and architectural excellence across the globe. Your ability to blend timeless elegance with regional character is truly remarkable. As the brand continues to explore new destinations and curated experiences, I wanted to introduce Saharsh Cabins — a provider of bespoke prefabricated modular structures designed specifically for premium hospitality. Our cabins are crafted to match high design standards while offering speed, flexibility, and sustainability — ideal for guest villas, wellness zones, pop-up retreats, or luxury staff housing in remote or underdeveloped locations. important.reason: New lead from Saharsh Cabins about modular cabins for Four Seasons.

important.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Commercial proposal - Dalmia - Belgaum, Kadapa
important.snippet: Dear Mr. Harshad, We have reviewed your Twin room offer and sharing the feedback and queries on design as under. 1. Clear height of rooms should be 9 feet. 2. Tiles in both room should be double charge vitrified tiles. 3. Please ensure insulation should be 100mm rockwool/glass wool. 4. Please confirm the density of rockwool/glass wool. 5. Please share the specifications of external wall cladding and partition walls. 6. AC provision to be provided in both room including supply and installation of electrical wiring, conduiting, drainage pipe & 16 A socket and switch. 7. Canopy to be provided over door (450mm) and windows (350mm). 8. GI doors are not acceptable request you to provide alternative in Wooden Flush door with Exterior grade Sunmica with frame. Based on above feedback and queries please share revised technical proposal with revised layout and separate commercial proposal by today, EOD. For 2 BHK we will share comments and feedback separately. Thanks /Regards, Anurag Sharma CEM-

important.subject: Meet Harshad; Saharsh
important.snippet: Hi Harshad, Writing to reconnect on the message I sent earlier. I am Chief Operating Officer at MDSPL, a BIM consulting company that provides Virtual Design and Construction solutions to architects, engineers, and construction firms around the world. At MDSPL, we work closely with companies like Saharsh, co-creating solutions that translate virtual planning into real-world construction, helping projects stay on schedule, within budget, and maintain high quality standards. From 3D modeling to 4D and 5D simulations, clash detection, quantity and cost estimation, construction sequencing, and 6D facility management, we cover the full project lifecycle. I can also share a strong use case from the Construction sector that illustrates how our approach has helped similar organizations achieve better results. Please let me know a good time to connect, or you can book directly via our calendar link.<https://calendly.com/rahul-d-mdspl/30min> Respectfully, Mukesh Jangir Chief Operating Officer Mer

important.subject: Connect; Meet Harshad
important.snippet: Hi Harshad, I came across your work in the Construction space in Mumbai and thought to reach out. I am Project Manager at MHXP (My Home Experience). We collaborate with companies in the Construction sector to bring their real estate projects to life before construction begins. Our platform turns CAD plans into real-scale 3D digital twins. Companies like Saharsh and their clients can see every detail, refine designs early, and make confident decisions. This reduces rework, avoids delays, and keeps projects on track. Each unit becomes an immersive virtual model, so buyers can quickly view layouts, finishes, and customization options. The models work on VR, mobile, tablet, and web, making the experience easy and engaging for everyone. We have delivered 29 projects for 17 builders, including Godrej Properties, Embassy Group, Sanjeevini Group, DLF, and ATMOS, combining design precision with a realistic, ergonomic experience for teams and clients. I’d love to discuss this in a 10-15 min call
important.reason: New lead/collaboration inquiry for Saharsh Cabins from an unknown sender.

important.snippet:
Dear Harshad,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Albert, and I am the Sales Manager at our company, which has been specializing in high-quality garden structures since 2020 in China. We offer a diverse range of products, including garden gazebos, pole tents, garden arches, pergolas, pavilions, and sun sails, all at competitive prices.

I believe there is a great opportunity for us to collaborate, and I would love to explore how we can work together. If you are interested, I would be happy to send you our e-catalogues so you can see our full range of offerings.

For more details and to view other exciting products, please feel free to visit our website. I look forward to your response and hope to establish a fruitful partnership soon.

Thank you for your time.

Best regards,

Albert
Sales Manager

You may share it with Chris. Thanks! *About us:* Luco Homes is a leading prefabricated manufacturer in India. We have traditionally built modular/ portable infrastructure for industrial and infrastructure clients for leading Indian conglomerates. Since covid, we have used our robust supply chain to build for outdoor hospitality and led multiple development projects in India. We are now increasingly focusing on the international markets and are very bullish about expanding in the US markets. We specialise in volumetric units which at the core are made of steel. important.subject: Re: NaNomad Pricing
important.snippet: Thank you all for the call — there was a lot of meaningful discussion. My biggest takeaway was the need to upgrade the chassis to ensure it can endure cross-country shipments. That shouldn’t be an issue. Regarding NOAH certification, I spoke with their sales rep about two years ago, and it seems like a solid program I can support. It would be great to have the unit certified by Harshad during the build, and I’m in favor of moving forward with that. As Harshad mentioned, as we fine-tune production, the workload on the U.S. side should naturally decrease. Before manufacturing additional units, I’d like to have clear visibility on pricing and margins as we move into larger-scale production. I’ll send the invoice to Brie and mail the Certificate of Origin to your office in LaGrange. Best regards, On Wed, Oct 15, 2025 at 8:54 AM Brieanna Horseman <brie@mscapescorp.com> wrote: > Is tomorrow possible? I am in the office today trying to have meetings > internally with our team if possible. > >
important.reason: Email mentions 'Harshad' and 'NOAH certification' related to cabins.

important.subject: Thank you Zach!
important.snippet: Dear Zach Harshad and I are on our way back to India. I wanted to express our gratitude to you towards what has been an amazing past 6 months of association between Luco and Mscapes. However, none of this would have been possible without you. Thank you for responding to my cold message around 14 months back. Thank you for every single piece of guidance ever since. Thank you for the detailed and critical feedback. We are very glad that we have a detailed list of actionables to make a better version of NaNomad going forward! This was one of the key things which we wanted to accomplish from our trip. Thank you for your humility and patience. We have come across a ton of experts who are often not approachable. It doesn't have to be that way - and you exemplify it! And thank you for 'The Real Slim Shady' performance! You absolutely are the real slim shady while all other slim shadys are just imitating! We look forward to working with you more in the future! Thank you, Zach! Cheers Punj & Ha
important.reason: Email from Punj Rajan mentioning Mscapes association and gratitude.

important.subject: Human Centric Lighting Design for Saharsh's projects
important.snippet: Dear Harshad Chandak, My name is Oleg Noskov, Director at iLiGHT International. As an international lighting consultancy firm, with global experience in around 40 countries of the world, including India, we use light as a strategic tool to make our clients' developments iconic and profitable. Since we're fully independent from manufacturers and suppliers our sole incentive is the success of Your project, by providing lighting solutions balanced from visual, technical and economical point of view. We collaborate strategecally both with world-class architectural practices like Zaha Hadid Architects and Gensler, respected architects and engineers working in Indian market, as well as Design build Contractor firms such as AECOM and Typsa. As an example, in our recent 'Canal Wharf' project, designed in cooperation with Gensler, we helped crafting an iconic identity for the 70-hectare development, turning a Developer's (Client) vision into a valuable asset. Lighting became a language of elega

important.subject: Re: Meeting Request – Luco Homes x Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities
important.snippet: It can be considered However, it will be a first for us. Of course, we have done 'hybrid' sites in India (e.g. the experience centre details which I had shared) but here we control the onsite work as well. That will not be the case for the US markets. So it will require significant coordination with any general contractor partner you may identify. In addition, we have often sensed that the general contractor job in the US ends up being significantly costly and time consuming. This erodes any potential benefit of decoupling in India. I will rely on your best judgement on this part. As Harshad had mentioned - we are always open to exploring new R&D challenges. Simply because we truly believe that any breakthrough opens up a lot of possibilities. Happy to understand what you have in mind and propose the best way forward On Sat, 26 Jul 2025 at 19:51, Christian Arnold <christian@clockwork-ad.com> wrote: > Understood. Since height will be a recurring challenge, have you > considered a 2 part
important.reason: Discusses 'Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities' and 'hybrid sites'.

important.subject: Re: Meeting Request – Luco Homes x Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities
important.snippet: Understood. Since height will be a recurring challenge, have you considered a 2 part solution, where the roof module is placed on top of the box once it arrives onsite? If this is possible we could explore a few design solutions together. christian arnold // founding principal <https://www.clockwork-ad.com/> kansas city // lisbon 816.694.0750 <http://www.clockwork-ad.com/> On Fri, Jul 25, 2025 at 11:14 PM Punj Rajan <punj@goluco.com> wrote: > Got it > > At this stage - I will be able to give you a broad estimate at $12-15k for > a unit (ex factory in India). This is because once we build one such custom > unit, we will get to understand a much more detailed & accurate estimate > (e.g. I am sure you understand that the choice of shower pan can vary the > pricing by thousands of dollars). > > Also, I'd recommend that the height of the unit is such that it can be > accommodated within a high cube container while shipping. Often this is > difficult to achieve when we are building cabins -
important.reason: Discusses 'Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities' and design solutions.

important.subject: Re: Meeting Request – Luco Homes x Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities
important.snippet: Got it At this stage - I will be able to give you a broad estimate at $12-15k for a unit (ex factory in India). This is because once we build one such custom unit, we will get to understand a much more detailed & accurate estimate (e.g. I am sure you understand that the choice of shower pan can vary the pricing by thousands of dollars). Also, I'd recommend that the height of the unit is such that it can be accommodated within a high cube container while shipping. Often this is difficult to achieve when we are building cabins - but can definitely be thought through for such units. This will save you significant shipping costs. There are volume based costing advantages which we pass on to the clients - but I don't think there will be anything significant when the number of units is close to 6. I hope this helps. Thanks.
important.reason: Discusses 'Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities' and cost estimates.

important.subject: Re: Meeting Request – Luco Homes x Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities
important.snippet: We can try to do that. Are you sure about the dimensions though? From the floorplan - it appears to be longer than 8' 6" - more like at least 12 - 13' Thanks Punj On Fri, 25 Jul 2025 at 21:14, Christian Arnold <christian@clockwork-ad.com> wrote: > Great, are you able to prepare a prelim cost estimate? > > christian arnold // founding principal > <https://www.clockwork-ad.com/> > kansas city // lisbon > 816.694.0750 > > <http://www.clockwork-ad.com/> > > > > > > > > > > > On Thu, Jul 24, 2025 at 9:29 PM Punj Rajan <punj@goluco.com> wrote: > >> Great to hear from you Christian! >> >> Yes of course. Shower pods/ saunas have been actively discussed in our >> ongoing conversations >> >> If the height is somewhere around 8'6'' - these can potentially fit >> within high cube containers while shipping. At 9' height, these will go on >> flat rack containers. 1 flat rack container can carry perhaps 6 of these >> units - so 6 does appear the ideal quantity to optimise on shipping >> >> Let me kno
important.reason: Discusses 'Outdoor Hospitality Opportunities' and cost estimates.

I kindly request that the

This policy ensures that you have the opportunity to build relationships, receive referrals and pass referrals to Members within each Business Category. To support you in keeping track of your engagement in our Chapter, you can review your reported absences in BNI® Connect (Reports > PALMS Attendance Report). If you believe this absence is incorrect, please talk to your Chapter’s Vice President. As you will remember, our commitment to each other as BNI Members is that we will not miss more than three meetings in a six-month period without a substitute. Attendance is the first step

Our general policy on attendance states “A Member is allowed three absences within a continuous six-month period. If a Member cannot attend, they may send a substitute; this will not count as an absence." Our records indicate this was your fourth absence in a continuous six-month period. important.snippet: To view this email as a web page, go to the following address: https://www2.bni.com/index.php/email/emailWebview?mkt_tok=MTY2LVNVTS03NDQAAAGavzpJkamA4U7jB2R5XgPoVCf8HeH80YwLHYF0YjNaz9JwThcUUmzYgFQ7nz8Qh2iUPTGNKvw2OMetDQVbHVVBaHX4ZKYRSRZZb2C31iNfseuHZUfHPw Please contact us about your fourth absence Hello Rakesh, As BNI Members we make a commitment to each other to not miss more than three meetings in a continuous six-month period. Our policies are designed to ensure Member Success. Building trusted relationships, where Members take a personal responsibility and commit to support one another, has proven to deliver a positive Member experience and the best potential for growth. Our general policy on attendance states “A Member is allowed three absences within a continuous six-month period. If a Member cannot attend, they may send a substitute; this will not count as an absence." Our records indicate this was your fourth absence in a continuous six-month period. important.subject: DCNEL Early Payment Solution // Contact Confirmation
important.snippet: Hi, Greetings of the Day, I'm trying to get in touch regarding DCNEL invoices. For context, we provide the platform to view approved invoices from your customer and request early payments (DCNEL Early Payment Program Overview). The program is often managed by a financial decision maker in coordination with someone who directly handles invoices. Are you the right person to talk to about this? I request your approval for Mr. Nikhil's resignation and to inform the BNI back office to remove him from BNI Connect, effective from May 1st. This will ensure that it does not affect the Chapter traffic lights of BNI Veer. important.reason: Email from a BNI Veer chapter member regarding BNI membership.

important.subject: Modular Cabins <>  Sustainable Solutions for StayVista’s Premium Villa Network
important.snippet: Hi, I hope this message finds you well. My name is Syed Vikhar. I’ve been following StayVista’s remarkable work in curating a portfolio of premium private villas across India, and I truly appreciate your commitment to offering personalized, high-end experiences in diverse and often remote destinations. As StayVista continues to scale across India’s varied geographies, I understand the operational and design challenges that come with delivering consistent quality and comfort — especially when working with independently owned homes or developing properties in scenic but infrastructure-limited regions. At Saharsh Cabins, we specialize in prefabricated modular structures that are sustainable, rapidly deployable, and tailored for premium guest experiences. Our units are ideal for supporting quick expansions, adding guest or staff accommodation, or even enhancing the luxury offerings on-site — all while minimizing environmental impact. With 5 manufacturing units located across India — Mumbai
important.reason: Email about modular cabins and sustainable solutions for hospitality.

important.subject: Sustainable Modular Solutions for Saffron Stays Expansion
important.snippet: Hi Teja, I hope this message finds you well. My name is Syed Vikhar. I’ve been closely following Saffron Stays’ inspiring journey in redefining hospitality through thoughtfully curated homes across India. Your focus on offering unique, immersive experiences in lesser-explored, often remote locations is both admirable and deeply aligned with the future of sustainable travel. As you continue to scale this distinctive model — one that celebrates exclusivity, regional charm, and personalization — I recognize the operational and infrastructural challenges that come with building in geographically diverse and sensitive areas. That’s where I believe Saharsh Cabins can offer valuable support. We specialize in prefabricated modular cabins that are not only quick to deploy but are also designed to be eco-conscious, durable, and highly adaptable to various terrains. These structures can help streamline development without compromising the natural beauty or character of the locations you work so h
important.reason: Email about modular solutions for hospitality expansion.

important.subject: Modular Cabins <> Sustainable Solutions for IHG Hotels & Resorts’ Expansion
important.snippet: Hi Elie, I hope this message finds you well. My name is Syed Vikhar. I have been following the remarkable journey of IHG Hotels & Resorts and truly admire your dedication to delivering world-class hospitality while embracing innovation and sustainability. As IHG continues to expand its footprint globally, I wanted to introduce you to Saharsh Cabins — a leading manufacturer of prefabricated modular structures. Our sustainable cabins are designed to reduce construction time, optimize costs, and significantly lower environmental impact by using eco-friendly materials and smart building techniques. With 5 manufacturing units located across India — Mumbai, Nagpur, Bangalore, Delhi, and Cuttack — we are well-positioned to support rapid deployment and consistent quality across various locations. I believe our modular accommodation units could seamlessly align with IHG’s growth strategy, especially in remote, seasonal, or fast-developing hospitality projects. I would be delighted to explore ho
important.reason: Email about modular cabins and sustainable solutions for hospitality.

Currently, we can facilitate bid bonds, advance payment, and performance bonds for projects based in India. important.snippet: Dear Vendor, Greetings! We are excited to announce that Dalmia Cement (North East) Limited has partnered with Mynd Fintech, a leader in working capital solutions, to offer you an opportunity to enhance your cash flow through our new Early Payment Program. Through the secure Mynd Fintech platform, you can now initiate early payment requests by offering a discount on your outstanding invoices with us. Once your discount is accepted, early payment will be processed on the next immediate payment cycle, and the funds will be credited to your existing bank account on record with Dalmia Cement (North East) Limited. Key Benefits of the Early Payment Program: * Immediate access to cash flow when needed * Fast and hassle-free payments * Early access to funds in exchange for a cash discount * Lower cost compared to traditional funding options like bank loans * No change to your existing payment terms Key Highlights - * Dalmia Cement North East Ltd will approve and upload your invoices into our pl

important.subject:
Re: Research Collaboration Proposal on Environmental Impact of Prefab
 vs. Conventional Construction
important.snippet: Hello Harshad For the research I will need a chance to visit your factory in Delhi. I want to know how much material is used for one typical module and where is it sourced from. I will do a life cycle assessment based on that data and compare it with a conventionally build module with standard materials. Regards Adarsh Remesh
important.reason: Research collaboration proposal on prefab construction, relevant to Saharsh Cabins.

important.subject:
Research Collaboration Proposal on Environmental Impact of Prefab vs.
 Conventional Construction
important.snippet: Dear Mr. Harshad Chandak, I hope this email finds you well. My name is Adarsh Remesh, and I am currently a fifth-year Bachelor of Architecture student at the School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi. We had the opportunity to meet during the D-Arc Convention held at Pragati Maidan on August 3rd, 2024. I am conducting research that aims to compare the environmental impact of prefabricated construction with that of conventional construction methods. I believe the data generated from this research could be valuable for your company’s marketing initiatives. I would love the opportunity to collaborate with you on this project, as it would allow me to gain deeper insights into current market trends and technological advancements. If this is of interest to you, please feel free to reach out. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to the possibility of working together. Best regards, Adarsh Remesh +91 7356537479 adarsh.remesh12arch20@spa.ac.in
important.reason: Research collaboration proposal related to prefabricated construction, relevant to Saharsh Cabins.

important.reason: Introduction from a BNI Veer member regarding 3D renderings, relevant to Saharsh Cabins.
